per_entity_results.generated: QUIC Protocol Performance Evaluation Across Implementations and Bandwidth Conditions
per_entity_results.reference: Performance Evaluation of QUIC Implementations with Limited Bandwidth

per_entity_results.generated: This dataset captures performance metrics of multiple QUIC protocol implementations under varying bandwidth constraints. The experiment measures transfer reliability, energy consumption, latency, and cryptographic handshake efficiency through synchronized client-server workflows. Repeated trials with different configurations generate logs, checksums, and timing data for statistical analysis. Evaluation scripts aggregate results to compare goodput, transfer time, and success rates, producing visualizations and TSV files for implementation comparison.
per_entity_results.reference: This experiment executes a single file download with client-server pairs of different QUIC implementations. The filesize, the implementations, and a bandwidth limit can be set to multiple values to execute the measurements with all combinations of the parameters. The amount of repetitions can also be set to repeat the same combination n times to ensure stable results. An inbuilt evaluation script aggregates results and creates a simple plot that compares different QUIC implementations.

per_entity_results.generated: This script configures and runs a QUIC server implementation to evaluate performance under variable bandwidth constraints, synchronizing with a client workflow to measure transfer reliability and energy consumption. It generates a test file, applies network rate limiting, captures server logs and cryptographic key material, and verifies data integrity via post-transfer hashing. Measurement data includes timing, energy metrics, and protocol-specific logs, with results uploaded for analysis. The implementation selection and configuration parameters are dynamically determined from external context.
per_entity_results.reference: Start a QUIC server with a given library. In advance, a testfile and a certificate is generated. If set, a bandwidth limit is applied with Linux tc. After a client signalizes a finished transfer, the server is terminated.

per_entity_results.generated: This script iterates a QUIC server benchmark with configurable implementation, file size, and bandwidth constraints, measuring energy consumption and logging operational data during each run. The implementation type, transfer size, and network throughput limits vary per iteration to evaluate performance characteristics under different conditions.
per_entity_results.reference: In each iteration, the QUIC client downloads a testfile from the QUIC server. All combinations for all values of implementation, bandwidth and filesize are run at least once, depending on the amount of repetitions set. For each run, the duration as well as logs from the respective endpoint are stored.
